steps.title: Identify What Makes a Claim Effective
steps.content: The task asks to both make and support a claim. The evidence that actually proves kale is a better source is the quantitative comparison in the notes: kale contains 120 mg of vitamin C per 100g, while blueberries contain 10 mg per 100g.

steps.title: Find the Choice That Uses This Evidence
steps.content: Choice B uses the quantitative evidence from the notes—showing kale contains 120 mg to blueberries' 10 mg per 100g—to directly support the claim. Choices A and D don't compare the fruits, while C makes the assertion without supporting evidence. That matches choice B — B is correct.
